free_text: Alpha-cluster break-up and reaction mechanism in
(p,alpha) reactions on light nuclei
Silicon surface barrier with 300 mu-m thick
The values obtained by normalization to data from
other publ.(see MONIT-REF in Subents) are in good
agreement with the values deduced from target-thickness
measurements.
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases
SD: Corrections in all Subents. BIB updated
The accuracy of data presentation has been revised.
SAMPLE moved to Subents 002-008. Subents 002-003;
005-007 were merged.
The boron targets consisted of self-supporting
highly enriched boron foils with thickness between
40 and 180 mu-g/cm2.
Error from graphic bars (statistical error)
Fig.2,3 from Nucl.Phys.,A398(1983)189
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
EN-SEC deleted. STATUS, ERR-ANALYS updated. MONITOR,
MONIT-REF, SAMPLE added. ANG-ERR -> ANG-ERR-D;
DATA-ERR -> ERR-S. Data re-digitized. Data from
Subent 003 added.
The boron targets consisted of self-supporting
highly enriched boron foils with thickness between
40 and 180 mu-g/cm2.
Fig.4 from Nucl.Phys.,A398(1983)189
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
EN-SEC, ERR-ANALYS deleted. STATUS updated. MONITOR,
MONIT-REF, SAMPLE added. EN-ERR -> EN-ERR-DIG;
DATA-ERR -> ERR-DIG. Data re-digitized. Value (E-LVL)
corrected  3.04 -> 2.95 MeV.
The sodium targets were prepared by evaporating
either metallic sodium or sodium-fluoride onto 20
mu-g/cm2 carbon foils. The sodium target thickness
was between 100 - 250 mu-g/cm2.
Error from graphic bars (probably
statistical error)
Fig.6 from Nucl.Phys.,A398(1983)189

The sodium targets were prepared by evaporating
either metallic sodium or sodium-fluoride onto 20
mu-g/cm2 carbon foils. The sodium target thickness
was between 100 - 250 mu-g/cm2.
